summary: Iteration 65: HYBRID STALENESS-ADJUSTED EXECUTION validation COMPLETE. All 25 backend tests passed. Verified: (1) ArbConfig has all new dynamic threshold params: staleness_edge_base_bps=15, staleness_edge_per_minute_bps=5, hard_max_stale_seconds=1800, liquidity_deep_threshold=2000, liquidity_mid_threshold=500, liquidity_buffer_thin_bps=15. (2) min_net_edge_bps lowered from 30 to 15 (absolute floor). (3) GET /api/strategies/arb/diagnostics returns dynamic_threshold_samples with calculated thresholds at various staleness/liquidity combos. (4) Hard stale reject working: data >1800s rejected with 'hard_stale_reject' reason. (5) Hard liquidity reject: min_liquidity=200. (6) Rejection log entries include all required fields: type, condition_id, question, total_cost, gross_edge_bps, fees_bps, slippage_bps, net_edge_bps, stale_age_s, liquidity, dynamic_min_edge_bps, reason. (7) Raw edges entries include stale_age_s and liquidity fields. (8) All existing arb endpoints still working.

Hard stale reject verified for >1800s - PASS

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 65 validated the HYBRID STALENESS-ADJUSTED EXECUTION system. The arb engine now uses dynamic thresholds based on data freshness (staleness) and liquidity depth. Key formula: dynamic_min_edge = staleness_bps(15 + age_s/60*5) + liquidity_bps(0/7.5/15) with floor at min_net_edge_bps(15). Hard reject rules: stale >1800s, liquidity <200. Currently seeing rejections due to stale weather market data (>4000s). 1 multi-outcome arb has already executed successfully (London weather). All rejection log entries include full diagnostic fields for debugging.

current_arb_stats.reason_for_rejections: Stale data (>1800s) in multi-outcome weather markets
